summary: Added a new C translation unit and header for ftCommon DamageFall, moving most of the 0x80090574-0x8009095C range out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s while leaving the later callback at 0x80090984 as an exported asm symbol. The PR fully matched most DamageFall routines according to the PR report, while ft_80090594 remained a partial match at 75.92%. It also exported/renamed several Damage helpers, tightened fighter/common-data struct types needed by the translation, fixed call signatures such as ft_8009665C(gobj), and updated report_score.py to score functions from expected ELF symbols.
what_changed: Created src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageFall.c and added it to obj_files.mk.

Removed the assembly bodies for ftCo_80090574, ft_80090594, ft_80090690, ft_80090718, ft_80090780, ftCo_DamageFall_Anim, ftCo_DamageFall_IASA, ftCo_DamageFall_Phys, and ftCo_DamageFall_Coll from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Kept the function at 0x80090984 in asm but promoted its local label .L_80090984 to global ftCo_80090984, and updated parasol damage-fall collision code to reference that symbol.
Added ftCo_DamageFall.h prototypes with address comments for the translated DamageFall functions.
Implemented simple wrapper routines such as ftCo_80090574 calling ft_80081DD4, ftCo_DamageFall_Phys calling ft_80084DB0, and ftCo_DamageFall_Coll calling ft_8008370C(gobj, ftCo_80090984).

Implemented ftCo_DamageFall_IASA as an interrupt chain using early RETURN_IF checks for ft_8009665C, ft_80095328, ftCo_800D7100, ftCo_800C3B10, ftCo_AttackAir_CheckItemThrowInput, ftCo_800D705C, ft_800CB870, ftCo_800C5DDC, and ftCo_800C5CD4, plus a stick-threshold transition through ft_800CC730.
Translated ft_80090690 to return a Vec2* shift or NULL, using Fighter_804D6530 and the DamageFall model-shift fields.
Translated ft_80090718 to clear queued damage effects/sounds via ft_80088148 and ft_800889F4.
Translated ft_80090780 to handle parasol status and otherwise change to motion state 0x26 with flags 0x18001, then call ftCommon_8007D468 and ftCommon_8007EBAC.
Left ft_80090594 with a MUST_MATCH asm version and a non-MUST_MATCH C version, reflecting the reported partial match.
Renamed the formerly static damage motion table ftItemPickup_803C5520 to exported ftCo_803C5520 and updated assembly references to the new symbol.
Exported formerly static Damage.c helpers as ftCo_Damage_CheckAirMotion, ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag, ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag, and ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold so other code can reference them.
Improved prototypes for dependencies used by DamageFall: ft_8008370C takes a gobj and callback, ft_8009665C takes a gobj, ftCo_800C5CD4 and ftCo_800C5DDC return bool, and ftCo_800CF4DC takes a gobj.
Updated Fighter_804D6530 from UNK_T to Vec2**.
Typed ftCommonData fields x168 and x16C as floats and exposed x210, x214, and x218 around the stick-threshold data used by DamageFall_IASA.
Typed Fighter damage fields x1900/x1904 as floats, x1908 as enum_t, and x190C as UNK_T.
Fixed existing callers in Link boomerang and Peach float code to pass gobj to ft_8009665C.
Changed ftcommon.c model-shift handling to check ft_80090690(fp, &shift) != NULL before applying shift.x and shift.y.
Updated tools/report_score.py to read function symbols from the expected ELF with pyelftools and invoke asm-differ for each function name; also adjusted sorting of report entries.
smart_moves: Split the work into a new ftCo_DamageFall.c rather than expanding ftCo_Damage.c, matching the subsystem boundary implied by the motion callbacks and making the object independently scoreable.
Used a MUST_MATCH asm fallback for ft_80090594 while providing a readable C attempt, preserving build correctness and documenting the remaining mismatch area.
Promoted .L_80090984 to ftCo_80090984 instead of leaving a local label referenced across code, making the collision callback reusable from both C and remaining asm.
Added precise return types and argument types for cross-file helpers before relying on them in the new translation, reducing dependence on UNK_PARAMS and avoiding accidental ABI mistakes.

Converted key struct fields only where the new C required them, such as ftCommonData floats and Fighter damage model-shift fields, giving the compiler enough type information without overclaiming broader struct knowledge.
Kept address comments in the new header, which makes future decomp work easy to map back to the original DOL addresses.
The PR report listed per-function scores, making the partial state explicit: all listed routines except ft_80090594 were reported as 100.00% matched.
decomp_lessons: When a decomp slice depends on helpers that were static inside another file, promote them with subsystem-prefixed names and add prototypes rather than duplicating logic.
For collision callbacks that remain in asm, turning local labels into globally named symbols is a practical bridge that lets C call the remaining asm safely.
A nonmatching but readable C version guarded away from MUST_MATCH is useful for preserving understanding while retaining the exact asm in matching builds.
Damage/action-state code often needs small signature corrections in unrelated character files because shared helper prototypes become stricter once translated.

Model-shift and hitlag code exposed concrete struct knowledge: p_ftCommonData offsets 0x168/0x16C are floats, offsets 0x210/0x214 are used for stick threshold/timer checks, and Fighter dmg offsets 0x1900/0x1904 store floor-normal floats.
Returning NULL versus a Vec2* in ft_80090690 was reflected in ftcommon.c by changing a truthy integer-style check to an explicit pointer check.
Per-function scoring tools are valuable for partial PRs: the report clearly separated fully matched callback/simple-wrapper functions from the remaining partial ft_80090594.
naming_conventions: New motion callback names use the ftCo_DamageFall_* pattern: ftCo_DamageFall_Anim, ftCo_DamageFall_IASA, ftCo_DamageFall_Phys, and ftCo_DamageFall_Coll.
Still-unknown functions keep address-based names such as ft_80090594, ft_80090690, ft_80090718, ft_80090780, and ftCo_80090984.

Formerly static ftCo_Damage helpers were exported with descriptive ftCo_Damage_* names: ftCo_Damage_CheckAirMotion, ftCo_Damage_OnEveryHitlag, ftCo_Damage_OnExitHitlag, and ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold.
The damage motion table was renamed from a misleading static ftItemPickup_803C5520 to exported ftCo_803C5520.
The new header guard used the project’s address-style pattern GALE01_090824.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the matched asm bodies from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s and replaced them with C in a new object file.
Kept ft_80090594 under #if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP) as inline asm, with a C implementation in the #else branch.
Used M2C_FIELD in ft_80090780 for an unresolved field at offset 0xE0 rather than over-typing the containing structure.
Used the RETURN_IF macro in ftCo_DamageFall_IASA to match the early-exit interrupt chain compactly.
Used ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) with p_ftCommonData->x210/x214 to model the stick-threshold branch in IASA.
Updated exact asm symbol references from .L_80090984@ha/@l to ftCo_80090984@ha/@l so C and asm agree on the callback symbol.

The PR report showed ft_80090594 at score 1517/6300, 75.92%, while ftCo_DamageFall_IASA, ft_80090780, ft_80090690, ft_80090718, ftCo_DamageFall_Coll, ftCo_80090574, ftCo_DamageFall_Phys, and ftCo_DamageFall_Anim were listed as 100.00%.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no explicit human review feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match ft_80090594 or rename it to a descriptive ftCo_DamageFall function.
Search for subsequent typing of Fighter_804D6530 from Vec2** into a named model-shift table type.
Look for PRs that remove M2C_FIELD usage around Fighter offset 0xE0 in ft_80090780.
Check later work on ftCo_80090984 to see whether the remaining collision callback asm was decompiled.
Find follow-up changes to report_score.py after its switch to pyelftools and expected ELF symbols.
